title: calyptridium parryi subsp. nevadense
intro: Calyptridium parryi subsp. nevadense, also known as Parry's calyptridium or Nevada windmills, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the family Portulacaceae. It is native to the western United States, particularly in the states of Nevada, California, and Utah. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, Calyptridium parryi subsp. nevadense is often used as a dried flower or root, and can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures.
